message:
fix: skip migrations on fresh DB init to prevent orphaned pages

On a fresh database, user_version is 0, so run_migrations() was
unnecessarily running the v3/v4 migrations that DROP+CREATE
idx_issues_ready — orphaning page 17 and causing doctor integrity
warnings.

Now detect fresh DBs before DDL runs, execute SCHEMA_SQL (which
creates everything at the current version), and stamp user_version
immediately, skipping migrations entirely.

Also fixes test helpers to use shared parse_created_id() and
future-proof date constants in JSONL rewrite tests.

Closes #221

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,8 +13,8 @@ OK schema.tables
 OK schema.columns
 OK db.recoverable_anomalies
 OK db.null_defaults
-WARN sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used
+OK sqlite.integrity_check
 OK counts.db_vs_jsonl: Both have 0 records
 OK sync.metadata: External changes pending import
 OK db.write_probe: No issues available for ID-REDACTED write probe
-WARN sqlite3.integrity_check: *** in database main ***; Page 17: never used
+OK sqlite3.integrity_check
